sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INTEGER, name VARCHAR(100), score FLOAT, created_at TIMESTAMP);
SELECT lcase(name) AS lower_name, score ** 2 AS squared_score FROM test_data WHERE score IS NOT NULL AND created_at IS NOT NULL;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT lcase(name) AS lower_name FROM test_data WHERE lcase(name) = 'alice') AS subquery1;
SELECT AVG(score ** 2) as avg_squared FROM test_data WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_data WHERE score > 90) GROUP BY lcase(name) HAVING avg_squared > 80;
SELECT a.id, a.name, a.score, lcase(b.name) AS lower_join_name FROM test_data a JOIN test_data b ON a.id = b.id WHERE score IS NOT NULL AND lcase(b.name) <> '' ORDER BY score DESC;
SELECT a.id, CASE WHEN score IS NULL THEN 'No Score' ELSE lcase(name) END AS name_with_score FROM test_data a WHERE created_at >= '2023-01-01' AND (score ** 0.5) > 9;
CREATE TABLE secondary_data (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, description VARCHAR(200));
INSERT INTO secondary_data (id, description) VALUES (1, 'Test'), (2, 'Another Test');
SELECT lcase(a.description) AS lower_description, b.id FROM secondary_data a JOIN test_data b ON a.id = b.id WHERE score IS NOT NULL;
SELECT lcase(name), score FROM (SELECT name, score FROM test_data WHERE score IS NOT NULL) sub WHERE lcase(name) LIKE '%a%';
